text: Which of the following statements about React component state are true when using functional components?

options.A: State updates are always synchronous.
options.B: The useState hook returns an array with the current state value and a setter function.
options.C: Multiple state updates within the same event loop tick are batched for performance.
options.D: Directly modifying the state object is the recommended practice for complex state.

text: What will be the output of the following Node.js code snippet?

options.A: Start, End, Next Tick 1, Next Tick 2, Promise 1, Promise 2
options.B: Start, Next Tick 1, Next Tick 2, Promise 1, Promise 2, End
options.C: Start, End, Promise 1, Promise 2, Next Tick 1, Next Tick 2
options.D: Start, Next Tick 1, Promise 1, Next Tick 2, Promise 2, End

text: A user reports that their request to /api/data sometimes hangs. You suspect an issue with middleware order or async operations. Which of the following could be potential causes?

options.A: A middleware function failed to call next() or send a response.
options.B: An asynchronous operation within a middleware didn't await its completion before calling next().
options.C: The route handler is defined before a necessary body-parsing middleware.
options.D: The server is running out of memory due to too many concurrent requests.

text: Which of the following best describes the "schema-less" nature of MongoDB?

options.A: It means you don't need to define any data structure for your documents.
options.B: It means documents in a collection can have different fields and structures.
options.C: It means MongoDB automatically infers and enforces a schema based on the first inserted document.
options.D: It means data validation is impossible in MongoDB.

text: Which of the following are core benefits of using a utility-first CSS framework like Tailwind CSS?

options.A: It eliminates the need for any custom CSS.
options.B: It promotes consistent design by using predefined scales and values.
options.C: It makes styling components faster by composing classes directly in HTML.
options.D: It automatically generates unique class names to avoid naming collisions.

text: Consider a React component that renders a list of items. Which techniques can help prevent unnecessary re-renders of individual list items when the parent component updates, but an item's props haven't changed?

options.A: Wrap the ListItem component with React.memo.
options.B: Use the useCallback hook for the handleClick function.
options.C: Ensure each ListItem has a unique and stable 'key' prop.
options.D: Pass a new object for 'item' prop on every render.

text: What will be the potential issues or outputs when running the following Node.js code?

options.A: The 'Counter' will increment indefinitely every 100ms.
options.B: The 'Counter' will stop incrementing after approximately 500ms.
options.C: The Node.js process will eventually crash due to a stack overflow.
options.D: The blocking operation will prevent any further 'Counter' logs after 500ms.

text: Your Express API handles user authentication. A security audit recommends preventing brute-force attacks on the login endpoint. Which Express middleware or technique is most suitable for this?

options.A: helmet for setting security headers.
options.B: cors for managing cross-origin requests.
options.C: express-rate-limit to restrict repeated requests.
options.D: body-parser for parsing incoming request bodies.

text: You are designing a MongoDB schema for a blog platform. Each post has many comments. Which of the following data modeling approaches are generally good practices for this relationship, considering read performance for displaying comments with a post?

options.A: Embed all comments directly within the post document if comments are few and rarely updated.
options.B: Use separate 'posts' and 'comments' collections, linking them with references (ObjectId).
options.C: Store only the comment count in the post document and fetch comments separately.
options.D: Duplicate the post title in each comment document to avoid joins.

text: Given the following Tailwind CSS classes, what will be the text alignment on a medium-sized screen (e.g., tablet)?

options.A: Text will be aligned to the left.
options.B: Text will be aligned to the center.
options.C: Text will be aligned to the right.
options.D: Text alignment will depend on the browser's default.

text: A component uses useEffect to fetch data. Why might the fetch request be triggered multiple times unexpectedly, and how can it be fixed?

options.A: The component is unmounted and remounted, often in StrictMode or during hot reloading.
options.B: The 'fetchData' function is being redefined on every render, causing a new function reference.
options.C: The 'userId' prop is changing, but it's not in the dependency array.
options.D: The empty dependency array `[]` means the effect runs after every render.

text: What is the correct way to handle an unhandled promise rejection in a Node.js application globally?

options.A: Using a try...catch block around the fetchData() call.
options.B: Adding a .catch() handler directly to the promise returned by fetchData().
options.C: Listening to the 'unhandledRejection' event on the process object.
options.D: Listening to the 'uncaughtException' event on the process object.

text: Which of the following are valid ways to define a route in Express.js that responds to HTTP GET requests for the path '/users'?

options.A: app.get('/users', (req, res) => { ... });
options.B: app.route('/users').get((req, res) => { ... });
options.C: app.use('/users', (req, res) => { ... });
options.D: app.post('/users', (req, res) => { ... });

text: Your MongoDB query `db.products.find({ category: "electronics", price: { $gt: 100 } }).sort({ name: 1 })` is slow. Which indexing strategies could significantly improve its performance?

options.A: Create a single-field index on `category`.
options.B: Create a compound index on `{ category: 1, price: 1 }`.
options.C: Create a compound index on `{ category: 1, price: 1, name: 1 }`.
options.D: Create a single-field index on `price`.

text: You're building a large application with Tailwind CSS. What are common challenges or best practices to consider for maintaining performance and clean code?

options.A: Manually purging unused CSS classes to reduce bundle size.
options.B: Using @apply for frequently repeated utility patterns to create semantic classes.
options.C: Avoiding custom configurations to ensure maximum compatibility.
options.D: Relying heavily on inline styles instead of Tailwind classes for dynamic styling.

text: What will be logged to the console when the following React component is rendered?

options.A: Effect ran: 0, Cleanup for: 0, Effect ran: 1
options.B: Effect ran: 0, Effect ran: 1, Cleanup for: 0
options.C: Effect ran: 0, Effect ran: 1
options.D: Effect ran: 0, Cleanup for: 1, Effect ran: 1

text: Consider the following two files. What statements are true regarding their interaction?

options.A: The output will be 1.
options.B: Each `require('./file1')` call creates a new, independent instance of `file1.js`.
options.C: Node.js caches modules after the first `require`.
options.D: The `count` variable is shared between `mod1` and `mod2`.

text: Your Express API uses `app.use(express.json())` and `app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true }))`. A client sends a POST request with `Content-Type: text/plain` and a JSON string in the body. What will happen?

options.A: The `req.body` will be an empty object `{}`.
options.B: The `req.body` will contain the parsed JSON object.
options.C: The request will result in a 400 Bad Request error.
options.D: You would need a custom middleware to parse `text/plain` as JSON.

text: Which MongoDB operation is used to add new documents to a collection?

options.A: db.collection.find()
options.B: db.collection.update()
options.C: db.collection.insertMany()
options.D: db.collection.deleteMany()

text: You want to add a custom color 'brand-blue' to your Tailwind CSS configuration. Which of the following steps are necessary?

options.A: Add 'brand-blue' directly to the `colors` object in `theme.extend`.
options.B: Run `npx tailwindcss init` to regenerate the config file.
options.C: Rebuild your CSS using `npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css`.
options.D: Define the color in a separate CSS file and import it.

text: You observe a React component re-rendering frequently, even when its props appear unchanged. The component receives a callback function and an object as props. What are common causes and solutions?

options.A: The callback function prop is being redefined on every parent render, breaking memoization.
options.B: The object prop is being created as a new reference on every parent render.
options.C: The component is not wrapped in `React.memo` or is a class component without `shouldComponentUpdate`.
options.D: The component's internal state is changing rapidly, causing its own re-renders.

text: Which statement accurately describes the Node.js event loop?

options.A: It's a single-threaded, blocking mechanism that executes all I/O operations sequentially.
options.B: It allows Node.js to perform non-blocking I/O operations despite being single-threaded.
options.C: It's a multi-threaded pool that handles all asynchronous tasks concurrently.
options.D: It's primarily responsible for garbage collection in Node.js.

text: You are building an Express API. Which of the following are good practices for structuring your routes and middleware?

options.A: Using `express.Router()` to modularize routes into separate files.
options.B: Placing global middleware like `express.json()` before route definitions.
options.C: Defining all route handlers directly in `app.js` for simplicity.
options.D: Using `app.use()` with a path prefix to mount a router.

text: You need to transfer funds between two accounts in a MongoDB database, ensuring atomicity (either both updates succeed or both fail). Which statements are true regarding this scenario?

options.A: MongoDB's single-document atomicity guarantees are sufficient for this operation.
options.B: You must use multi-document transactions, which require a replica set deployment.
options.C: If one update fails, you need to manually roll back the other successful update.
options.D: Transactions ensure that other clients cannot see intermediate states of the data.

text: In React, what is the primary purpose of the 'key' prop when rendering lists of elements?

options.A: To provide a unique identifier for CSS styling.
options.B: To help React efficiently identify which items have changed, are added, or are removed.
options.C: To enable event delegation for list items.
options.D: To pass data from parent to child components.
